phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — shadow-shuriken executes edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your shadow-shuriken executes beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 your shadow-shuriken executes beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your shadow-shuriken executes out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your shadow-shuriken executes beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Your shadow-shuriken executes out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control on first item — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Respect window: at two-plus items the wall-frost poke control beat your shadow-shuriken executes cleanly. Trade only off Anivia's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q through the wave's gaps on his last-hits — energy refunds on hits mean accuracy IS your mana bar.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W opens the double-shuriken angles: shadow wide, Q the scissor — the geometry chips half bars by 3.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E joins: W-E-Q strings slow and shred. Pick the angle his minion cover doesn't block.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Poke the scissors on cooldown and bank energy before every real string — the lane bleeds to geometry.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Death Mark online — anyone below 60% in lane is now a math problem with a three-second answer.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: R-strings delete squishies through pots. Hunt the chip thresholds.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control own this window — your shadow-shuriken executes don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Death Mark online — anyone below 60% in lane is now a math problem with a three-second answer.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: R-strings delete squishies through pots. Hunt the chip thresholds.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Zed picks: mark the carry, swap through the peel, and let the death note collect the paperwork.
